passage.title: Sky Watch Chart
passage.text: Our group keeps a weather board near the window. During homeroom, Mina looks at it before the first bell. The weather board has picture labels for hot, cold, and rainy weather for the day's job. Our teacher asks us to read the sky words together. Sometimes we compare today's label with yesterday's label and notice one small change. We like the weather board because it helps us talk about the sky clearly.

questions.prompt: What is this passage mostly about?
questions.rationale: The whole passage explains how the weather board is used.
The first sentence says the weather board is near the window.

questions.prompt: When does Mina use the weather board?
questions.rationale: The second sentence says Mina uses it during homeroom.

questions.prompt: What does the weather board have?
questions.rationale: The third sentence says the weather board has picture labels.

questions.prompt: What does compare mean in the fifth sentence?
questions.rationale: Compare means to look at two things and think about them together.

questions.prompt: What does notice mean in the fifth sentence?
questions.rationale: Notice means to see and pay attention to something.
